Appearance
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
List of squads for men's international team for FIFA World Cup 2026

The 2026 FIFA World Cup is an ongoing international football tournament being held in Canada, Mexico, and the United States, from June 11 to July 19, 2026. The 48 national teams involved in the tournament were required to register a squad of up to 26 players, including three goalkeepers. Only players in these squads will be eligible to take part in the tournament.[1]

A provisional release list of between 35 and 55 players per national team had to be submitted to FIFA by May 11, 2026, one month prior to the opening match of the tournament. The release lists were not made public by FIFA. From the preliminary squad, the final list of at least 23 and at most 26 players (at least three of whom shall be goalkeepers) per national team had to be submitted to FIFA by June 1, ten days prior to the opening match of the tournament.[2] The squad lists were published by FIFA the following day.[3] In the event that a player on the submitted squad list suffers from an injury or illness prior to his team's first match of the tournament, that player could be replaced at any time up to 24 hours before their first match. The team doctor and the FIFA General Medical Officer have to both confirm that the injury or illness is severe enough to prevent the player from participating in the tournament. Replacement players are limited to the preliminary list.[1]

The position listed for each player is per the official squad list published by FIFA. The age listed for each player is as of June 11, 2026, the first day of the tournament. The numbers of caps and goals listed for each player do not include any matches played after the start of the tournament. The club listed is the club for which the player last played a competitive match prior to the tournament.[note 1] The nationality for each club reflects the national association (not the league) to which the club is affiliated. A flag is included for coaches who are of a different nationality to their team.

Group A

Czech Republic

Coach: Miroslav Koubek

The Czech Republic announced a 54-man preliminary squad on May 12.[4] The squad was reduced to 29 players on May 21.[5] They announced their final squad on May 31.[6]

Group B

Canada

Coach: Jesse Marsch

Statistics

Age

Players

  • Oldest: Cristiano Ronaldo (41 years, 126 days)[125]
  • Youngest: Gilberto Mora (17 years, 240 days)[126][125]
Goalkeepers
  • Oldest: Craig Gordon (43 years, 162 days)[127][125]
  • Youngest: Mladen Jurkas (18 years, 247 days)
Captains
  • Oldest: Cristiano Ronaldo (41 years, 126 days)[125]
  • Youngest: Alphonso Davies (25 years, 221 days)

Coaches

  • Oldest: Dick Advocaat ( Curaçao, 78 years, 257 days)[128]
  • Youngest: Julian Nagelsmann ( Germany, 38 years, 323 days)

Total1248100%913 (73.16%)89 (7.13%)
  • None of the squads are made up entirely of players from the countries' domestic leagues. The Qatar and Saudi Arabia squads have 25 players from their respective leagues.[125]
  • The Cape Verde, Curaçao, DR Congo, Ivory Coast, Senegal and Uruguay squads don't include any players employed by clubs in their home country.[125]
  • Uruguay league is the only domestic league represented at the tournament which doesn't provide any player for their national team.
  • The Canada and New Zealand squads both include players employed by domestic clubs, but no players employed in the Canadian or New Zealand domestic league systems. All Canadian domestic players play for Canadian teams in the American Major League Soccer; all New Zealand domestic players play for New Zealand teams in the Australian A-League Men.
  • The Netherlands squad has the most players from a single foreign federation, with 15 players employed in England.
  • Of the countries not represented by a national team at the tournament, Italy's league provided the most squad members, with 71.[125]
  • The lowest leagues on a domestic pyramid to be represented at the tournament are National League, the English fifth tier, and Oberliga, the German fifth tier. They are represented by New Zealand player Tommy Smith (Braintree Town) and Haiti player Josué Duverger (Cosmos Koblenz), respectively.

Notes

  1. ↑ This is the club a player was last able to play for during the previous season in the event a player did not play a competitive match.
  2. ↑ Includes players from Swansea City and Wrexham, which are based in Wales but play in the English league system.
  3. ↑ Includes five players from the third tier and one player from the fifth tier.
  4. ↑ Includes one player from the fifth tier.
  5. ↑ Includes players from Monaco, which is based in Monaco but plays in the French league system. Monaco is not a FIFA member.
  6. ↑ Includes one player from the third tier.
  7. ↑ Includes players from Toronto FC and Vancouver Whitecaps FC, which are based in Canada but play in the American league system.
  8. ↑ Includes players from Auckland FC and Wellington Phoenix, which are based in New Zealand but play in the Australian league system.

  1. 1 2 "Regulations – FIFA World Cup 26" (PDF). FIFA. March 19, 2026. Archived (PDF) from the original on April 6, 2026. Retrieved March 19, 2026.

  5. ↑ "Reprezentace před MS: širší nominace se třemi nováčky i navrátilcem Hložkem" [Representation before the World Cup: extended nomination with three newcomers and returnee Hložek] (in Czech). Football Association of the Czech Republic. May 21, 2026. Retrieved May 21, 2026.
  6. ↑ "Finální nominace reprezentace na mistrovství světa 2026" [Final nominations for the 2026 World Cup] (in Czech). Football Association of the Czech Republic. May 31, 2026. Retrieved May 31, 2026.

With the same sacrifice of those who came from the bottom and reached far. This is our squad, and we will show the world that this is Ecuador.] (in Spanish). Ecuadorian Football Federation. May 31, 2026. Retrieved May 31, 2026 – via Instagram.

  101. ↑ "منتخب النشامى يختتم تحضيراته في عمان.. ويغادر إلى سانت غالن غدا" (in Arabic). Jordan Football Association. May 27, 2026. Retrieved May 27, 2026.
  102. ↑ "The Jordanian national team's squad for the 2026 World Cup finals has been announced – Video". royanews.com. June 2, 2026.
  103. ↑ "أمنيات السلامة والشفاء العاجل للاعب إبراهيم صبرة، والعودة إلى الملاعب في أقرب وقت بعد أن أظهرت الفحوصات الطبية إصابته بتمزق في أربطة الكاحل للقدم اليسرى أثناء التدريبات، ما يستدعي خضوعه لبرنامج علاجي وتأهيلي خلال الفترة المقبلة، واستبعاده من قائمة النشامى لكأس العالم 2026" [Wishing safety and a speedy recovery for player Ibrahim Sabra, and a return to the fields at the earliest opportunity, after medical examinations revealed his injury with a tear in the ligaments of his left ankle during training, which necessitates his undergoing a treatment and rehabilitation program in the coming period, and his exclusion from the Nashama squad for the 2026 World Cup.] (in Arabic). Jordan Football Association. June 5, 2026. Retrieved June 5, 2026.
  104. ↑ "قرر الجهاز الفني للنشامى بقيادة المدرب جمال سلامي ضم اللاعب محمد أبو غوش إلى قائمة النشامى لكأس العالم 2026" [The national team’s technical staff, led by head coach Jamal Sellami, has decided to include the player Mohammad Abu Ghoush in the Nashama squad for the 2026 World Cup] (in Arabic). Jordan Football Association. June 8, 2026. Retrieved June 5, 2026.
